# Fei-Fei Li — Co-Founder & Chairperson, World Labs [Auto-Generated] 

**Twitter/X:** [@drfeifei](https://x.com/drfeifei)
**Substack:** [drfeifei.substack.com](https://drfeifei.substack.com/)
**Background:** Stanford CS Professor, Co-Director Stanford HAI, creator of ImageNet, author of *The Worlds I See*

## Key Themes

- **Spatial intelligence** is the next frontier beyond language models
- **LLMs are "wordsmiths in the dark"** — they lack grounding in the physical world
- **Democratization of AI** — every individual should have agency to impact this technology
- **Science over science fiction** — AI policy should be grounded in evidence
- **Human-centered AI** — "There's nothing artificial about AI. It's created by people."
- **Skills over degrees** — hiring for learning velocity

## [Auto-Pulled] Attributed Quotes

### Spatial Intelligence as the Next Frontier
> "Spatial intelligence will be the defining challenge of the next decade."
> — TED Talk, 2024 ([TED.com](https://www.ted.com/talks/fei_fei_li_with_spatial_intelligence_ai_will_understand_the_real_world))

> "Spatial Intelligence is a critical piece of the AI puzzle."
> — [Twitter/X](https://x.com/drfeifei/status/1790811274684584257)

> "The ability to understand, to reason, to interact with and to navigate the real 3D, 4D physical world is the foundation."
> — [PYMNTS Interview, January 2026](https://www.pymnts.com/artificial-intelligence-2/2026/fei-fei-li-says-ai-progress-now-depends-on-physical-context/)

> "What is a really really hard problem to work on in AI? My own answer is Spatial Intelligence."
> — [Twitter/X, World Labs launch](https://x.com/drfeifei/status/1834584286932181300)

### LLMs and Their Limitations
> "Today's AI is still like a very eloquent wordsmith who's writing in the dark... They have no idea how the physical world works."
> — TED Talk, 2024

> "Three essential capabilities for building AI that creates with a storyteller's imagination, navigates with a first responder's fluency, and reasons about space."
> — [Substack: From Words to Worlds](https://x.com/drfeifei/status/1987891813387292725)

### Democratization and Human-Centered AI
> "There's nothing artificial about AI. It's inspired by people. It's created by people. And most importantly, it impacts people."
> — Various appearances

> "Every individual should feel they have the agency to impact this technology."
> — [Bloomberg Weekend Interview, November 2025](https://www.bloomberg.com/features/2025-fei-fei-li-weekend-interview/)

> "It's not AI's future, it's humanity's future."
> — [Issues in Science and Technology, Spring 2024](https://issues.org/interview-godmother-ai-fei-fei-li/)

### Policy and Governance
> "We need to govern on the basis of science, not science fiction."
> — [AI Action Summit, Paris, February 2025](https://techcrunch.com/2025/02/08/ai-pioneer-fei-fei-li-says-ai-policy-must-be-based-on-science-not-science-fiction/)

> "Never before have we stood at such an extraordinary intersection of scientific possibilities and urgent global responsibility."
> — [UN Security Council Briefing, December 2024](https://press.un.org/en/2024/sc15946.doc.htm)

### Hiring and Talent
> "How quickly you can superpower yourself with new tools" matters more than degrees.
> — [Fortune, December 2025](https://fortune.com/2025/12/12/fei-fei-li-stanford-professor-godmother-ai-college-degrees-skills-talent-ceo/)

### On ImageNet's Legacy
> "They handed out ImageNet-branded pens to drum up interest" at the original CVPR 2009 poster presentation.
> — Various retellings of the ImageNet origin story

## Background
- PhD Stanford (advised by Pietro Perona)
- Co-Director Stanford HAI (Human-Centered AI Institute)
- Creator of ImageNet (15M images, 22K categories) — catalyzed the deep learning revolution
- Queen Elizabeth Prize for Engineering (2025, shared with Hinton, LeCun, Bengio)
- Memoir: *The Worlds I See* (2023)
